feedback.text: The framework set out by the Digital Fairness Act provides an opportunity to prevent video game publishers from permanently disabling or destroying video games they have sold to customers. Games that depend heavily on online infrastructure should have an end-of-life plan to make sure consumers can still run them when the publisher decides to withdraw support after release. There are very few successful examples of this being handled responsibly, many games have already been lost and many more currently available games are at risk.
